Uplink

Computer-Assisted Reporting Newsletter, 1990–2007

Browse Topics Search

Investigative Methods (139)

← All topics

GAS PUMP INSPECTIONS MAKE SPEEDY CAR STORY

Lee Davidson January/February 2007
Investigative Methods

Lee Davidson conducted a fast-paced investigation into the accuracy of gas pump measurements across Utah, revealing that 1 in 25 pumps were faulty.

NY POWER AGENCY GIVES $1 MILLION TO LEADERS' PET PROJECTS, CHARITIES

Michelle Breidenbach January/February 2007
Investigative MethodsHealth & Environment

An investigation into the New York Power Authority revealed $1 million in donations annually to executives' favored charities, funded by taxpayer money.

DATA REVEALS CITY WORKERS' BONUS PAY

Matt Stiles January/February 2007
Government & PoliticsInvestigative MethodsData & Databases

The story uncovers a payroll-padding scandal in Houston's city council's administrative office, revealing that four employees improperly received $143,000 in bonus payments. Furthe…

Capitol aide's two jobs leave money trail

Thomas Peele November - December 2006
Investigative MethodsGovernment & PoliticsBusiness & Economy

Thomas Peele investigates Steven J. Ding, detailing his dual roles and the financial trails they left, including potential violations of congressional rules.

Duff Wilson November - December 2006
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsTechnology

Duff Wilson provides insights into accessing records for profiling uncooperative sources, highlighting useful online tools.

ER gridlock exposed

Augusta Brennan Jones May-June 2006
Investigative MethodsCrime & JusticeGovernment & Politics

Investigation into Maryland ERs revealed significant delays and highlighted a crisis in emergency healthcare.

Rolling hazards in rail cargo

David Danelski May-June 2006
Investigative Methods

Investigation into freight train derailments revealed ineffective track inspections and other issues that endangered residents.

Benjamin Lesser July-August 2004

An investigation into voting rights violations in Teaneck, New Jersey, revealing racially polarized voting patterns and the potential for federal intervention.

Marc Chase March 2004
Investigative Methods

Investigative MethodsGovernment & PoliticsBusiness & Economy

An investigation into no-bid government contracts in Tennessee, revealing potential corruption and cronyism.

Debbie Cenziper June 2000

The daily grind

Neil Reisner May 2000
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsHealth & Environment

Driving computer-assisted reporting requires preparation, akin to practicing for a concert. It involves anticipating and analyzing available data to enhance reporting on deadline.

David Wethe March 2000
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsHealth & Environment

A report on the growth of computer-assisted reporting (CAR) globally, highlighting issues of access to electronic information and successful projects in various countries.

Brant Houston December 1999
Data & DatabasesInvestigative Methods

This article discusses the challenges journalists face when attempting to obtain data through FOIA requests, offering strategies to negotiate with agencies for better access.

Naming the unknown

Dawn Fallik June 1999
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsPublic Safety

Sampley's investigation led to the exhumation and identification of the Vietnam-era Unknown Soldier using military databases, revealing him to be Lt. Michael Joseph Blassie.

THE PRICE OF CONCENTRATED COMPETITION

Christopher H. Schmitt April 1999
Investigative Methods

An analysis of Nasdaq trading records reveals investors incur higher costs when trading is concentrated among fewer dealers, impacting millions of investors.

Doug Stanley March 1999
Investigative Methods

The Tampa Tribune uncovered that a large nursing-home chain planned to evict more than 50 poor residents in Tampa. This initiated a broader investigation revealing systemic issues …

David Burnham and Susan Long December 1998
Investigative MethodsGovernment & Politics

Challenges the FBI's secrecy and lack of accountability, using data analysis and the Freedom of Information Act to access and evaluate FBI enforcement actions.

David Heath December 1998

Investigation into the neglect and inadequate health care provided to prisoners by for-profit health services, exemplified by the death of inmate Calvin Moore.

Dave Davis February 1991
Investigative Methods

Newsday Report Pays Off

Tom Braden January 1991
Investigative MethodsBusiness & Economy

An investigation by New York Newsday revealed that the city owes $275 million in tax overpayments to property owners, highlighting the ease of uncovering bureaucratic errors using …

Datacore News

December 1990
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsPublic Safety

Beginning in January, Datacore will finally be ready for use. Datacore is an on-line database service that can be used by reporters to access government records.

Plugging In

Susan Drumheller December 1990
Investigative MethodsTechnologyData & Databases

Every other month, Uplink asks an individual or organization that is just beginning to use computer- assisted reporting to share their problems and victories for "Plugging In.

MICAR Library News

November 1990
Health & EnvironmentInvestigative MethodsData & Databases

MICAR makes available to reporters a library of data stored on 9-track computer tapes. A complete list of these tapes can be promptly mailed toanyone upon request.

George Landau October 1990
Investigative MethodsCrime & JusticeGovernment & Politics
